commit.message:
Update app/lib/gtag.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feature: Preline UI v4 migration (#361)

* feat: migrate UI to Preline UI v4.1.2 with interactive playground showcase

- Upgrade Preline UI from v3.0.1 to v4.1.2
- Migrate all views from Fomantic UI to Preline/Tailwind CSS
- Redesign playground as interactive component gallery
- Add new showcase components: stats, accordion, modal, tabs, dropdown, tooltip, timeline, stepper, alerts
- Fix 7 runtime bugs (tab-content ParseError, File/Select missing methods, media-library var mismatch, etc.)
- Update version cache-busting strings in LaravoltBladeDirectives

* deleted raw assets

* updated assets with Preline v4.1.2

* fix: Preline UI showcase - z-index stacking, modal, buttons, labels, cards

- Fix modal content behind backdrop by moving overlays to @push('body')
- Fix dropdown/timeline overlapping sticky header with proper z-index
- Use inline z-index styles (Tailwind JIT arbitrary values not compiled)
- Rewrite button showcase with 8 colors across solid/soft/outline/ghost variants
- Rewrite label/badge showcase with solid/soft/outline + indicator dots
- Rewrite card showcase replacing Fomantic icons with SVGs
- Add theme toggle (dark/light) to public layout header
- Add loading spinner with inline @keyframes for button states
- Expose showcase on public route /platform/preline

* fix: add null check for Auth::user() in RestfulButton policy check

* feat: add Phase 1 Form Enhancement components

- AdvancedSelect: searchable, multi-select dropdowns with Preline HSSelect
- ComboBox: type-ahead autocomplete search input
- SearchBox: global search input with keyboard shortcut support
- InputNumber: numeric stepper with +/- buttons
- Datepicker: calendar picker powered by HSDatepicker

* feat: add Phase 2 Data Presentation components

- Chart: interactive charts (bar, line, area, pie, donut) via ApexCharts
- Datatable: sortable, searchable data tables
- TreeView: hierarchical tree view with expand/collapse

* feat: add Phase 3 Content & Upload components

- Editor: WYSIWYG rich text editor
- FileUpload: drag-and-drop file upload with preview
- FileUploadProgress: upload progress indicator

* feat: add Phase 4 UX Polish components

- AvatarGroup: stacked avatar display
- ButtonGroup: grouped action buttons
- InputGroup: input with prefix/suffix addons
- RangeSlider: range input with value display
- LegendIndicator: color-coded status indicators
- ContextMenu: right-click context menus
- StrongPassword: password strength meter
- DragDrop: sortable drag-and-drop lists via SortableJS

* feat: register 19 Preline UI components in PlatformServiceProvider

Register all Phase 1-4 component classes and update view aliases
for AdvancedSelect, ComboBox, SearchBox, InputNumber, Datepicker,
Chart, Datatable, TreeView, Editor, FileUpload, FileUploadProgress,
AvatarGroup, ButtonGroup, InputGroup, RangeSlider, LegendIndicator,
ContextMenu, StrongPassword, and DragDrop.

* feat: add datepicker.js and select.js to Preline script loading

Load separate Preline plugin bundles for HSDatepicker and HSSelect
which are not included in the main index.js bundle.

* feat: update showcase page with Phase 1-4 component sections

Include all four phase showcase partials for complete Preline UI
component demonstration page.

* chore: rebuild assets.zip with Preline v4.1.2 dist files

* wip

* Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

commit.message:
feat: implement C bindings for zsys transport module

Add 10 header+source files for the transport C FFI layer:
- http/base.h+c: BaseHttpClient vtable, response/headers types, URL builder, header merger
- http/retry.h+c: Retry policy with exponential backoff, jitter, status checking
- http/client.h+c: Concrete HttpClient with retry, JSON/text/binary helpers, download
- wss/wss.h+c: WebSocket placeholder
- transport.h+c: Module init, backend listing

All compile clean with gcc -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -pedantic.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: security hardening for findings 2-10 (#87)

* feat: security hardening for findings 2-10

- Fix .gitignore casing for appsettings.development.json
- Restrict Swagger UI to development environment only
- Use constant-time password comparison (CryptographicOperations.FixedTimeEquals)
- Add HTTP security headers middleware (X-Frame-Options, CSP-adjacent, etc.)
- Add same-origin CORS policy
- Replace ex.Message with generic error messages in API responses
- Gate debug endpoints behind admin authentication
- Add rate limiting (5 req/min) on admin login endpoint
- Replace static Dictionary with ConcurrentDictionary + 10-min TTL for OAuth state
- Add OAuthStateTtlTests for state eviction and one-time-use verification

* fix: address PR review feedback

- SecureCompare uses SHA-256 hashes to prevent length-based timing leaks
- Path traversal prevention in GetAdminJsonPath
- Extracted StateTtl constant in DaikinOAuthService
- Added TimeProvider for deterministic TTL testing
- Added TTL expiration and eviction tests with FakeTimeProvider
- Updated test XML doc to match coverage
- Added AddCors/UseCors same-origin CORS policy
- AdminRepository.CheckAdminAccess uses constant-time comparison

* fix: sanitize storage directory to resolve CodeQL path-injection alerts

- Added GetSafeStorageDirectory() rejecting .. traversal sequences
- LoadAdminJson/SaveAdminJson construct paths inline from sanitized dir
- Resolves CodeQL cs/path-injection alerts

* Update Program.cs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR review feedback

- CORS: restrict to exact scheme+host+port (not all localhost ports)
- Rate limiter: partition per remote IP instead of global shared limiter
- CSP: skip for /swagger paths so Swagger UI works in development
- Tests: make EvictExpiredStates test async, assert state3 survives eviction with mock
- Plan: update CORS step to reflect actual AddCors/UseCors implementation

* fix: address PR review feedback

- Refactor LoadAdminJson/SaveAdminJson to use GetAdminJsonPath, removing dead code duplication

---------

Add functionality to enable star tracker constraints to be included in the scheduler (#27)

* Add pre and post sequence overhead parameters to integration calculations (VDA/NIRDA)

* Add pre and post sequence overhead parameters to integration calculations (VDA/NIRDA)

* feat: roll-aware visibility with star-tracker earth-limb keep-outs

- Add solar power fraction helpers and roll sweep functions to roll.py
- ScheduleProcessor sweeps candidate rolls (0-360° in roll_step increments)
  filtered by min_power_frac, selecting the roll that maximises visible time
- Roll sweep only activates when st_* constraints are explicitly set;
  vanilla ScheduleProcessor(tle1, tle2) behaves as before
- Fix atomic extend/shrink in _fix_visibility to prevent overlaps when
  remaining duration < min_sequence_duration
- Thread precomputed rolls through _fix_visibility and validate_visibility
- Update pyproject.toml deps to match pandoravisibility (notebook ^7.5.4,
  black ^26.1.0)
- Add test_gap_filling.py (18 tests) and roll sweep tests in test_roll.py
- Add roll-aware-visibility-example.ipynb demo notebook

* docs: fix solar power fraction docstrings — Y is panel hinge axis, not normal

The formula sin(arccos(|dot(Y,sun)|)) is correct and matches
pandoravisibility's get_visibility_best_roll exactly. The docstring
incorrectly stated +Y was the 'solar-panel normal' with Lambert's
cosine law. On Pandora, +Y is the panel hinge axis: power = 1.0
when Y ⊥ Sun (panels face-on), power = 0.0 when Y ∥ Sun (edge-on).

* Update solar panel power fraction threshold and add integration tests for VDA and NIRDA sequences

- Changed the minimum acceptable solar-panel power fraction from 0.8 to 0.7 in the ScheduleProcessor class.
- Added a new integration test file `test_integration_counts.py` to validate the functionality of VDA and NIRDA integration calculations.
- Implemented multiple test cases to ensure correct handling of overheads and frame/integration counts for both VDA and NIRDA sequences.

* fix: correct precomputed_rolls type hints to Dict[str, Optional[float]]

* fix: remove jupyter-contrib-nbextensions (incompatible with notebook v7, breaks mkdocs build)

* Apply suggestion from @Copilot

Breaking up a comment into two lines to meet line length requirements (Black)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* changing min_sequenc_duration

* bumping version

* perf: precompute Sun vectors once per times array in roll sweep

compute_mean_solar_power was called inside the candidate-roll loop
and iterated over time samples calling get_sun() per sample, giving
O(N_candidates x N_times) ephemeris lookups per target.

Fix:
- Add _sun_vectors(times) which calls get_sun() once for the whole
  times array and returns an (N, 3) float64 array via vectorised
  numpy ops (no Python loop).
- Add _mean_solar_power_from_sun_vecs(ra, dec, roll_deg, sun_vecs)
  which computes the mean power fraction from a precomputed sun_vecs
  array using a single batched dot-product (sun_vecs @ y_payload).
- Refactor compute_mean_solar_power to delegate to both helpers,
  removing the per-sample Python loop (public API unchanged).
- find_best_roll_for_target now precomputes sun_vecs = _sun_vectors(times)
  once before the candidate loop and calls _mean_solar_power_from_sun_vecs
  inside, reducing ephemeris cost from O(N_candidates x N_times) to
  O(N_times).

103 tests pass, black/flake8 clean.

* changing default post sequence to 120 s

---------

Co-authored-by: trevorfoote <tof2@cornell.edu>

* Address code review: fix duration arg, NIRDA guard ordering, init validation, and add tests

- _update_payload_parameters_sequence: pass sequence.duration (TimeDelta)
  instead of sequence.duration.to(u.us) so both helpers receive the correct
  type
- _update_NIRDA_integrations: use the duration argument instead of re-reading
  sequence.duration; move missing-params guard before int() conversions so
  VDA-only sequences return cleanly
- __init__: validate overhead parameters are Quantity/TimeDelta with time units,
  raising TypeError for plain numbers and ValueError for wrong-unit Quantities
- tests/test_integration_counts.py: add TestUpdatePayloadParametersSequence
  (8 tests covering overhead-reduces-count and overhead>=duration->0 via
  wrapper) and TestScheduleProcessorOverheadValidation (4 tests)

Allow converter frontend options to be used in liquid files (#2932)

Converter frontend options (FillerDukes, StaticDeJure, LegionConversion,
etc.) are now available in `.liquid` template files alongside existing
CK3 mod flags through a unified `GetLiquidFlags()` method.

## Changes

- **Configuration.cs**: Added `GetLiquidFlags()` method that combines
CK3 mod flags and converter options into a single collection. Made
`GetCK3ModFlags()` and `GetConverterOptions()` private helper methods.
- **WorldOutputter.cs**: Updated `CopyBlankModFilesToOutput()` to accept
unified liquid flags
- **World.cs**: Replaced all uses of `GetCK3ModFlags()` with
`GetLiquidFlags()`
- **CulturesOutputter.cs** and **FileTweaker.cs**: Updated to use
`GetLiquidFlags()`
- **Tests**: Added unit tests validating liquid template integration
with converter options

## Format

Uses colons (`:`) as separator in format `OptionName:choiceValue`:
- `FillerDukes:0`, `FillerDukes:1` (count vs duke)
- `StaticDeJure:1`, `StaticDeJure:2` (dynamic vs static)
- `LegionConversion:No`, `LegionConversion:SpecialTroops`,
`LegionConversion:MenAtArms`

**Note**: Due to DotLiquid limitations, options with colons must be
accessed using square bracket notation in templates.

## Example

```liquid
{% if ['FillerDukes:1'] %}
  generate_filler_dukes = yes
{% elsif ['FillerDukes:0'] %}
  generate_filler_counts = yes
{% endif %}

{% if tfe and ['StaticDeJure:2'] %}
  # Combine mod flag (direct access) with converter option (bracket notation)
  tfe_static_dejure_mode = yes
{% endif %}
```

All major converter options (HeresiesInHistoricalAreas, UseCK3Flags,
SkipDynamicCoAExtraction, SkipHoldingOwnersImport) are now accessible.
CK3 mod flags (`tfe`, `roa`, `vanilla`) can still be accessed directly
without brackets.

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Allow converter frontend options to be used in liquid
files</issue_title>
> <issue_description>Original Concept:
> 
> > Would it be possible to have the converter setup so that it
automatically reads what converter frontend options you chose and adds
them to some .liquid dictionary, like what's done for the CK3 mods? For
example, for the filler rulers option, it would see which option you
chose and add either `FillerDukes_0` or `FillerDukes_1` (or
`FILLER_DUKES_0`/`FILLER_DUKES_1` if it's easier to do the displayName
instead) to the .liquid dictionary, which could then be used in .liquid
files the same way `roa`/`tfe`/`vanilla`/etc. is used now. Obviously,
the FillerDukes option probably wouldn't need to be used in any liquid
files, but some of the gamerules I've had to make so far could be made
into converter frontend options, and whenever we run into issues like
that in the future where we want to give the player some choice in how
things are handled, it'd be easier to handle it that way instead of
going through the process of making a gamerule or hardcoding part of the
converter.
> 
> Initial Comments from IhateTrains:
> > That should work, although I'd prefer to use a format like `<option
name>:<selected choice name>` for the values, for example
`FillerDukes:0` (BTW, the `name` values in option choices don't need to
be numbers, so they could be more descriptive). Would need to test if
the colons can be used. And yeah, I also prefer converter frontend
options to CK3 game rules.</issue_description>
> 
> ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section)
> 
> <comments>
> </comments>
> 


</details>

commit.message:
ci: implement P1+P2 pipeline improvements

P1 - Fix NOW:
- fix: sync VERSION file to 1.0.9 (was 1.0.5)
- ci: add sync-version job to release.yml (auto-updates VERSION after each release)
- ci: expand pytest in ci.yml to include 8 validated root-level test files
- ci: delete duplicate lint.yml (flake8 already ran in ci.yml)
- ci: delete redundant docker.yml (ci.yml now handles docker build+verify)

P2 - Quality improvements:
- ci: add black + isort checks to ci.yml lint job
- style: run black + isort (--profile black) across entire codebase (185 files)
- ci: add --cov-fail-under=20 coverage threshold to ci.yml
- ci: add installer + portable E2E tests to build.yml (after installer build)
- ci: add docker build + container start verification to ci.yml
- chore: add setup.cfg with [isort] profile = black

Branch protection (manual): Settings -> Branches -> main -> require 'CI / Lint' and 'CI / Test'

commit.message:
Add Skia core CVE tracking to security audit skill (#3556)

Extend the security-audit skill to treat Skia core as a first-class
dependency. Previously the skill only audited third-party libraries;
Skia itself was invisible to Component Governance (no standalone CPE).

Changes:
- SKILL.md: NVD query workflow using chrome_milestone comparison
- report-template.md: Skia-specific table formats and assessment guide
- cgmanifest.json: chrome_milestone and upstream_merge_commit fields
- dependencies.md: Skia CVE tracking methodology documentation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: show -p overrides in print-env and improve unknown-field config errors (#7701)

`print-env` only showed environment variables, omitting `-p` CLI
overrides. Additionally, when an env var or `-p` arg referenced an
unknown config field (e.g.
`TARI_BASE_NODE__P2P__TRANSPORT__TOR__PROXY_BYPASS_ADDRESSES_TEST`), the
app exited with an opaque serde error and no guidance.

## Changes

### `print-env` now includes `-p` args
- `print_env_vars()` signature changed to
`print_env_vars(config_overrides: &[(String, String)])` — both env vars
and `-p` overrides are displayed, with sensitive keys (PASSWORD, SECRET,
KEY, SEED) masked
- `--print-env` CLI flag in `minotari_node` and
`minotari_console_wallet` passes `cli.common.config_property_overrides`
to the function
- Interactive `print-env` shell command gains a
`config_property_overrides: Vec<(String, String)>` field on
`CommandContext`, populated from `cli.common.config_property_overrides`
in `run_base_node_with_cli`

### Better unknown-field error UX
- Added `ConfigurationError::is_unknown_field_error() -> bool` to avoid
`to_string()` allocation when detecting the error type
- `ApplicationConfig::load_from` failures in both node and wallet now
print an actionable hint:
  ```
⚠️ Configuration error: an environment variable or -p override
references an unrecognized config field.
Run with --print-env to inspect active environment variables and -p
overrides.
  ```

### Example output for `--print-env` / `print-env`
```
Tari-related environment variables:
  TARI_BASE_NODE__GRPC_ENABLED=true
  TARI_NETWORK=esmeralda

Config property overrides (-p args):
  base_node.grpc_enabled=true
  test.stuff=true
```

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>update envVars usage and help feedback</issue_title>
> <issue_description>shell envVars, like ```MINOTARI_NODE_ENABLE_GRPC```
as reported in the help screen, is not working.
> following variation also not working: 
> ```
> MINOTARI_NODE__GRPC_ENABLED=true
> MINOTARI_NODE__GRPC_ADDRESS=/ip4/0.0.0.0/tcp/18142
> MINOTARI_NODE_GRPC_ENABLED=true
> MINOTARI_NODE_GRPC_ADDRESS=/ip4/0.0.0.0/tcp/18142
> MINOTARI_BASE_NODE_GRPC_ENABLED=true
> MINOTARI_BASE_NODE_GRPC_ADDRESS=/ip4/0.0.0.0/tcp/18142
> MINOTARI_BASE_NODE__GRPC_ENABLED=true
> MINOTARI_BASE_NODE__GRPC_ADDRESS=/ip4/0.0.0.0/tcp/18142
> ```
> What did work was
> ```
> TARI_BASE_NODE__GRPC_ENABLED=true
> TARI_BASE_NODE__GRPC_ADDRESS="/ip4/0.0.0.0/tcp/18142
> ```
> 
> Would be great if we could resolve the 
> ```
> "Invalid value for `base_node`: invalid type: string" ... "expected a
sequence"
> ```
> for an envVar like
> ```
>
TARI_BASE_NODE__P2P__TRANSPORT__TOR__PROXY_BYPASS_ADDRESSES='["/ip4/192.168.101.50/tcp/12997","/ip4/192.168.101.54/tcp/12997","/ip4/192.168.101.50/tcp/12998","/ip4/192.168.101.54/tcp/12998"]'
> ```
> </issue_description>
> 
> ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section)
> 
> <comments>
> <comment_new><author>@leet4tari</author><body>
> Might be an idea to add a warning if the old style envs are used with
suggestion to change.</body></comment_new>
> </comments>
> 


</details>

Replace NurseDashboard, PharmacistDashboard, and AdminDashboard with full implementations

- NurseDashboard: vitals logging with offline support, medication round tracker,
  patient detail cards, emergency alerts, shift inspection checklist, activity log
- PharmacistDashboard: prescription management, drug interaction alerts, inventory
  management with low-stock detection, daily bill history with revenue summary
- AdminDashboard: interactive stat cards with hover details, bed occupancy bar chart,
  monthly revenue line chart, staff management with dept filter, compliance checklist
